data.title: UCB SA reports 1H results
data.description:
• UCB SA press release (OTCPK:UCBJF): 1H revenues increased to €3.49 billion.
  • Underlying profitability (adjusted EBITDA) also progressed, reaching 29.6% of revenue.

data.title: Exxon set to sign offshore gas exploration deal with Libya. embassy says
data.description:
Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) is on the verge of signing an offshore gas exploration deal in Libya, according to the U.S. Embassy in Tripoli, which would mark the company's return to the country after a decade-long absence.

Separately, the Libyan government has launched its first major oil and gas licensing round since 2007, offering 22 onshore and offshore exploration blocks.

The head of Libya's National Oil Corporation said nearly 40 multinational companies, including Exxon (NYSE:XOM), Chevron (CVX), TotalEnergies (TTE) and Eni (E) have shown interest in bidding for blocks.

Libya currently produces 1.3M-1.4M bbl/day of oil, but the NOC aims to ramp up output to 2M bbl/day within the next three years, provided sufficient foreign investment is secured, and Libya could hold more than 90B barrels of untapped oil and gas reserves, positioning the country as one of the most promising yet underexplored energy frontiers in North Africa.

Key have begun to resume operations in Libya: BP and Eni restarted drilling activities in the Ghadames Basin in 2024, and oilfield services company Weatherford returned in 2025 after more than a decade-long absence.

data.title: AGCO shares surge after earnings beat expectations, outlook raised
data.description:
Shares of AGCO (NYSE:AGCO) jumped 9% Thursday morning, hitting their highest level since May 2024, after the agricultural equipment maker reported second-quarter results that topped Wall Street forecasts and raised its full-year outlook.

The company posted adjusted earnings of $1.35 a share, beating analysts’ estimates of $1.08. Revenue came in at $2.6 billion, surpassing the $2.50 billion consensus, though down nearly 19% from a year earlier, partly due to the prior-year divestiture of AGCO’s Grain & Protein business and weaker industry demand.

Despite softer tractor and combine sales globally, Chief Executive Eric Hansotia highlighted the company’s execution in a tough environment.

“AGCO achieved solid second-quarter results with deliberate execution in the areas we can control despite a challenging global agricultural environment marked by weak farm economics and delayed purchasing decisions in several parts of the world,” Hansotia said in a statement. He added that strong free cash flow and inventory reductions reflected “meaningful progress” on cost discipline and restructuring initiatives.

Regional results showed sharp declines in North America, where sales fell 33% year-over-year, with softer demand for high-horsepower tractors and sprayers. Europe and South America were more resilient but still down, while Asia-Pacific experienced a mid-single-digit drop.

Looking ahead, AGCO now expects full-year 2025 net sales of about $9.8 billion and earnings of $4.75 to $5.00 a share, up from prior guidance, citing stable pricing and ongoing efficiency efforts.

Hansotia said the company sees rising farmer interest in precision agriculture and sustainable technologies even as traditional equipment purchases slow, particularly in North America and Europe amid volatile commodity prices and trade uncertainty.

data.title: Align Technology plunges on results miss, weak outlook
data.description:
Align Technology (NASDAQ:ALGN) shares tanked after it posted second quarter revenue and adjusted profit below expectations, while also estimating a sequential fall in Q3 revenues.

The company reported a 1.9% fall in quarterly worldwide revenues to $1.01B, below consensus of  $1.06B. It also posted an adjusted profit of $2.49 per share, missing analysts' estimate by $0.08.

The stock of the company is trading down about 31% in early trading.

Align also expects a Q3 worldwide revenues to be in the range of $965M to $985M, down sequentially from Q2, as it sees macroeconomic conditions affecting demand for its teeth aligners.

The Tempe, Arizona based firm said it will take a series of actions in the second half of fiscal 2025 to streamline operations and reallocate resources to meet profitability objectives.

It expects to reduce global workforce, optimize manufacturing footprint and dispose of certain manufacturing capital assets as steps to restructure the business.

"We expect these actions will incur one-time charges of approximately $150 million to $170 million in the second half of 2025, primarily for the write-down of assets, accelerated depreciation expense, and restructuring charges," Align said in a statement.

data.title: Hammerson reports 1H results
data.description:
• Hammerson press release (OTCPK:HMSNF): 1H Growth in gross rental income:  up 5% like-for-like, up 11% including acquisitions
  • Acquisition of remaining 50% interest in Bullring and Grand Central for £319m1 and associated equity placing
  • Dividend growth of 5% and upgraded earnings outlook
  • Like-for-like gross rental income up 5% and like-for-like net rental income up 4%, driven by active asset management and strategic focus on high quality landmark destinations
  • Total gross rental income up 11%, net rental income up 10%, following successful deployment of capital: £321m over nine months at average 8.5% destination yield
  • EPRA earnings of £48m, 9.9p per share (HY24: £50m, 9.9p) ahead of expectations - dividend increase of 5% to 7.94p reflects confidence in earnings growth trajectory
  • Portfolio valuation up 11% to £3.0bn – net revaluation gain of £26m is the first portfolio gain since HY17
  • Also separately announced today – unconditional agreement to buy remaining 50% interest in Bullring and Grand Central at 7.7% blended topped-up NIY2 and a 4% discount to June 2025 book value, and associated equity placing
  • Opportunities to unlock further value, with disciplined capital allocation strategy to enhance returns for shareholders
  • EPRA earnings guidance for FY25 raised to c.£102m (from c.£95m) - on track to achieve medium term financial

data.title: UK regulator says Amazon, Microsoft impacting cloud competition
data.description:
U.K.'s competition regulator said that Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) are hurting competition in the cloud computing industry and called for a review into their market dominance under the country’s new tech rules.

The Competition and Markets Authority said Microsoft and Amazon Web Services, or AWS, "have been generating sustained returns from their cloud services substantially above their cost of capital in cloud services for a number of years."

"The Inquiry Group’s final report disregards clear evidence of robust competition in the UK’s IT services industry, which cloud computing has revolutionised by dramatically reducing costs and expanding customer choice and flexibility. The action proposed by the Inquiry Group is unwarranted and undermines the substantial investment and innovation that have already benefitted hundreds of thousands of UK businesses," said an AWS spokesperson in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

"It risks making the UK a global outlier at a time when businesses need regulatory predictability for the UK to maintain international competitiveness. We will continue to engage constructively with the CMA as they consider their next steps," the AWS spokesperson added.

The agency also said that Microsoft’s licensing practices are adversely impacting the competitiveness of AWS and Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Google in the supply of cloud services, particularly in competing for customers that buy cloud services which use the relevant Microsoft software as an input.

"As a result, Microsoft faces weaker competitive constraints from AWS and Google, its most significant competitors, which is reducing competition in cloud services markets," the CMA added.

This gives rise to adverse effects on competition, or AEC, in cloud services in the U.K., according to the regulator.

"The CMA Panel’s most recent publication misses the mark again, ignoring that the cloud market has never been so dynamic and competitive, with record investment, and rapid, AI-driven changes. Its recommendations fail to cover Google, one of the fastest-growing cloud market participants. Microsoft looks forward to working with the Digital Markets Unit toward an outcome that more accurately reflects the current competition in cloud that benefits UK customers," said a Microsoft spokesperson in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha. 

The watchdog added that market concentration, which is particularly high in the case of infrastructure as a service or IaaS, and barriers to entry and expansion are features that have enabled the two largest providers, Microsoft and AWS, to hold significant unilateralmarket power in the cloud services markets and to earn returns above the cost of their capital over a sustained period.

The CMA noted that these features give rise to an AEC, in cloud services in the U.K. because it is harder for alternative cloud suppliers to enter and grow in these markets and customers face a limited choice of suppliers and products.

The agency said that technical and commercial barriers are features that give rise to an AEC in cloud services in the U.K. "These barriers lock customers into their initial choice of provider, which may not reflect their evolving needs and limits their ability to exercise choice of cloud provider," the CMA added.

As per the CMA, Microsoft and AWS, each has a high share of supply at about 30% to 40% in the IaaS market in 2024. The platform as a service, or PaaS, market is also concentrated; Microsoft’s share of it has slightly increased to 20% to 30% and AWS’ share has remained stable at 10% to 20% since 2020.

The third largest provider, Google, has a much lower share of supply in both IaaS and PaaS markets at [5% to10%] but this has grown since 2020, the agency noted.

The CMA recommended that the "CMA Board use its digital markets powers to prioritise commencing SMS investigations to consider designating the two largest providers Microsoft and AWS with strategic market status (SMS) in relation to their respective digital activities in cloud services."

Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers, or DMCC, Act was  introduced in the U.K. with the aim to prevent anti-competitive behavior in digital markets.

The CMA said that it will keep under review possible options for further SMS designation investigations, and it expects that options will be considered by the CMA Board in early 2026.

Update: The story was updated with statements from Microsoft and Amazon's AWS.

data.title: Rolls-Royce GAAP EPS of 52.15p, revenue of £9.49B, raises FY25 guidance
data.description:
• Rolls-Royce press release (OTCPK:RYCEY): 1H GAAP EPS of 52.15p.
  • Underlying operating profit rose by 50% to £1.7bn with a margin of 19.1%, reflecting the impact of our strategic initiatives, operational effectiveness, and performance management
  • Revenue of £9.49B (+7.1% Y/Y).
  • Full year 2025 guidance raised: Expects £3.1bn-£3.2bn underlying operating profit and £3.0bn- £3.1bn free cash flow

data.title: Air Products tops estimates despite lower volumes; raises outlook for full-year EPS
data.description:
Air Products and Chemicals (NYSE:APD) reported fiscal third-quarter earnings that beat Wall Street expectations Thursday, as higher pricing, favorable currency and cost productivity offset lower helium demand and the effect of project exits.

The industrial gases company posted adjusted earnings of $3.09 a share, topping analysts’ forecast of $2.99. Revenue came in at $3.02 billion, edging past the consensus estimate of $2.99 billion and up 1% year-over-year. GAAP earnings were $3.24 a share, up 4% from the same quarter last year.

While the company faced a 4% drop in volumes -- linked to a previously announced LNG business sale, softer global helium demand, and exited projects -- pricing gains and cost reductions helped cushion results. Adjusted operating income was $741 million, roughly flat compared to last year.

“The Air Products team delivered solid results this quarter that exceeded guidance and were higher than last year on a comparable basis, excluding the impact of the LNG sale,” Chief Executive Eduardo Menezes said in a statement. “We are staying focused on our cost productivity efforts, pricing, operational excellence and capital discipline.”

Regionally, Asia and Europe showed volume growth and improved margins, while the Americas segment was pressured by lower helium sales and maintenance-related costs. Income from Middle East and India affiliates declined 4%.

Looking ahead, Air Products (NYSE:APD) tightened its full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $11.90 to $12.10, and forecast fourth-quarter adjusted EPS of $3.27 to $3.47. Capital expenditures for fiscal 2025 are expected to reach about $5 billion, as the company continues investing in clean hydrogen and industrial gas infrastructure.

data.title: HeidelbergCement reports 1H results
data.description:
• HeidelbergCement press release (OTCPK:HDLMY): 1H Group revenue rose by 4.0% in comparison with the previous year to €10,398 million (previous year: €9,994M).
